title: Taco Salad
subtitle: with Chipotle Seitan and Cashew Cheese Sauce
description: Nacho cheese sauce that's packed with protein? Yes, please! Our cashew "cheese" sauce takes only a couple minutes to whisk up, and we do it last minute to ensure it's hot for serving. If you want to reheat, simply whisk in a bit warm water. If you prefer not to fry the tortilla strips, brush the tortillas lightly with oil before slicing and then roast them in the oven at 400°F until they are browned and crisp.

steps.title: PREPARE THE TOPPINGS
steps.text: Remove the husk from the corn and cut the kernels off the cob. Thinly slice the radishes. Roughly chop the romaine.

steps.title: CRISP THE TORTILLAS
steps.text: Place a medium nonstick skillet over medium-high heat and add ½ cup vegetable oil. Slice the corn tortillas into thin strips. Once the oil is hot, add the tortilla strips and cook until golden brown and crisp, about 1 minute. Transfer to a paper towel lined plate and sprinkle immediately with salt. Transfer the hot oil to a heatproof container and return the skillet to medium-high heat.

steps.title: COOK THE SEITAN
steps.text: Add the Sweet Earth chipotle seitan to the hot skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until browned in places, about 4 to 5 minutes. Season seitan with salt and pepper.

steps.title: BUILD THE SALAD
steps.text: Place the chopped romaine on large plates and top with the corn, radishes, black olives, pickled jalapeños, and chipotle seitan. Halve the lime and squeeze half over the taco salads.

steps.title: MAKE THE CHEESE SAUCE
steps.text: Add the cashew butter, nutritional yeast, turmeric, paprika, ½ tsp salt, and ½ cup cold water to a small saucepan. Bring sauce to a simmer while whisking continuously, until just thickened, about 30 seconds to 1 minute. Once thickened, add juice from remaining lime, stir, and remove from heat.

steps.title: SERVE IT UP
steps.text: Drizzle taco salads with some of the cashew cheese sauce and top with tortillas strips. Serve any remaining sauce on the side. Dig in!

tips: No need to wipe out the empty skillet.
